title: Role Mailbox Postgres repository + CRUD with transactional lifecycle enforcement
goal: Implement the Postgres-backed RoleMailboxRepository over the MT-176 schema. Provides the only authority-bearing CRUD path for threads and messages, with lifecycle transitions enforced inside the transaction and append-only message history. This is the substrate every downstream X.1/X.2 mailbox primitive uses for durable state.
expected_diff_shape: New Rust module src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/repo.rs providing `RoleMailboxRepository` with create_thread / get_thread / update_thread_lifecycle / append_message / list_thread_messages / list_threads_by_state / dead_letter_message methods; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_repo_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-176 typed primitives + Postgres migration must be merged so the repo can target the schema.
implementation_notes: Per CX-503R Postgres-only authority and spec §5.7 ProcessOwnershipLedger pattern, the repo wraps a `sqlx::PgPool` and exposes async methods. `update_thread_lifecycle(thread_id, requested_state, by_role) -> Result<RoleMailboxThread, MailboxError>` MUST execute the transition check inside a `BEGIN; SELECT ... FOR UPDATE; UPDATE ...; COMMIT` block to prevent races (cluster X spec subsection [ADD v02.176] forbids races between parallel models). `append_message(thread_id, message) -> Result<RoleMailboxMessage, MailboxError>` MUST: (a) check thread lifecycle_state is in an accepting state (not archived/expired/resolved), (b) assign Uuid v7 message_id if not provided, (c) insert with delivery_state=`queued`. `dead_letter_message(message_id, reason)` transitions delivery to `dead_lettered` and writes an audit row. `list_threads_by_state(state, limit, offset)` is the validator query surface (mirrors spec §5.7.4 pattern). No method writes outside the transaction. All errors typed via `thiserror::Error`. Integration tests use a Postgres test container (use testcontainers-rs or the KERNEL-003 fixture pattern in tests/common.rs): (a) create thread, append 3 messages, list them, expect chronological order; (b) attempt illegal lifecycle transition, expect `MailboxError::InvalidTransition`; (c) parallel update_thread_lifecycle calls — one wins, one returns `MailboxError::Conflict`; (d) append_message against archived thread rejects.
risk_if_missed: Without a transactional repo, parallel executors (folded WP-1-Role-Mailbox-Executor-Routing-Claim-Lease-v1 intent) race on the same thread; lifecycle transitions silently become non-deterministic; the cluster X preservation slice loses its 'one lawful ownership layer' invariant.
validator_focus: All lifecycle mutations atomic via SELECT FOR UPDATE; append_message rejects on terminal lifecycle states; concurrent transitions produce exactly-one-winner semantics; no SQLite anywhere (CX-503R).

implementation_or_hardening_notes: Normalized handoff expires_at_utc to Postgres microsecond precision before bundle hashing.

note: MT-177 targeted repository lifecycle round trip passed.

postgres_test_url_live_evidence_20260525.summary: Role Mailbox V1 live Postgres suites passed, including repository lifecycle, lease, backpressure, handoff, and exporter coverage.
residual_risks: Supporting evidence only; Integration Validator verdict remains PENDING.
Focused Postgres/live-kernel commands were run, not a full workspace cargo test.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Concurrency test launches >= 8 parallel tasks attempting the same illegal transition; exactly one succeeds, others receive Conflict or InvalidTransition.
Repo refuses to construct against a SQLite connection (CX-503R) via type bound on PgPool only.
Append-only invariant tested: no method exposes a way to delete or rewrite a message row.

implementation_supporting_evidence_20260.summary: MT-177 Role Mailbox Postgres repository + CRUD transitioned PENDING -> CLAIMED -> READY_FOR_VALIDATION. The existing src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox_v1/repo.rs (RoleMailboxRepository on sqlx::PgPool, ensure_schema, create_thread, get_thread, update_thread_lifecycle with SELECT ... FOR UPDATE, append_message with TerminalState guard, list_thread_messages, list_threads_by_state, dead_letter_message, count_pending_messages_for_role, MailboxError typed via thiserror) was reviewed and accepted; no in-place edits needed to the repo module. A dedicated MT-177 adversarial test file was added at tests/role_mailbox_repo_tests.rs (the owned-files path) covering: (a) CX-503R compile-time PgPool-only constructor guard, (b) MailboxError variant Display distinctness, (c) thread_id and message_id Uuid v7 mint-site enforcement (HBR-INT-008), (d) lifecycle terminal-state snapshot, (e) RoleId persistence round-trip, (f) append-only negative-surface check (no delete/update_body), plus Postgres-gated adversarial tests: thread CRUD round-trip with chronological message order, eight-parallel-caller illegal-transition race (exactly-one-winner), append-after-archived rejects TerminalState, append-against-missing-thread rejects NotFound, update_thread_lifecycle on missing thread rejects NotFound, FK ON DELETE CASCADE proven by direct DELETE, concurrent eight-writer append preserves (created_at, message_id) order, transactional rollback leaves no visible state, list_threads_by_state filters by lifecycle, dead_letter_message audits reason and keeps the row (append-only), count_pending_messages_for_role isolates by to_roles membership.

out_of_scope_edits.change: Added [[test]] block declaring role_mailbox_repo_tests with required-features=['test-utils'] mirroring the role_mailbox_v1_tests pattern. Necessary because the test binary cannot enforce required-features without a declared [[test]] entry; Cargo auto-discovery falls back to defaults which omits explicit test-utils gating.

implementation_supporting_evidence_20260.contract_deviation_note: MT-177 expected_diff_shape and owned_files name `src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox/repo.rs`, but the existing folded implementation lives under role_mailbox_v1/repo.rs (the same PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT class flagged in MT-176 implementation_supporting_evidence_20260521.contract_deviation_note). No new product code under role_mailbox/ was created — that would duplicate the authoritative implementation. Operator/Integration Validator decision needed on path-shape drift vs. the actual role_mailbox_v1/ tree.

proof_commands.note: 7/7 default tests pass; 11 Postgres-gated tests #[ignore]-ed pending POSTGRES_TEST_URL.

proof_commands.command: cargo test --features test-utils --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --test role_mailbox_v1_tests --test role_mailbox_repo_tests -- --nocapture --test-threads=1
proof_commands.note: Adjacent role_mailbox_v1_tests still pass alongside the new MT-177 test binary; no regression from the Cargo.toml [[test]] addition.

residual_risks: 11 Postgres-gated adversarial tests (eight-parallel-caller race, append-after-archived, FK CASCADE, transactional rollback, etc.) require POSTGRES_TEST_URL to actually validate the in-database SELECT ... FOR UPDATE semantics. Without the live-Postgres run the contract's red_team.minimum_controls #1 is structurally satisfied but not executionally verified — same Postgres-gating class as MT-190 / MT-197.
PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT: owned_files name role_mailbox/repo.rs but the implementation is under role_mailbox_v1/repo.rs (folded preservation slice). Integration Validator decision needed.

Cargo.toml [[test]] addition is out-of-owned-files; operator waiver requested. The alternative — relying on Cargo auto-discovery — would silently default to feature set [] and bypass the test-utils requirement that other role_mailbox_v1 tests depend on.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: RoleMailboxRepository correctly executes lifecycle transitions and message appends inside SELECT ... FOR UPDATE transactions on a PgPool-only type bound, rejects terminal-state appends, and chains lease/handoff methods through the same transaction discipline.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: RoleMailboxRepository correctly executes lifecycle transitions and message appends inside SELECT ... FOR UPDATE transactions on a PgPool-only type bound, rejects terminal-state appends, and chains lease/handoff methods through the same transaction discipline.
